name: mcp-builder
purpose: 4-phase guide for building high-quality MCP servers (Python FastMCP or Node/TypeScript) with evaluation harness, covering design principles, implementation, code review, and evaluation-driven testing.
notes: No version/compatibility/tools frontmatter fields — minimal frontmatter (name, description, license only). No skills: field. No _shared/ dependencies. Phase 1.3/1.4 instruct loading live URLs via WebFetch — external network dependency baked into workflow. Python scripts (connections.py, evaluation.py) are execution helpers for evaluation harness. shadcn-components.tar.gz in scripts/ is a bundled asset — unusual binary in a skill dir. LICENSE.txt present. NOT ported to JASON-OS.

name: repo-analysis
purpose: Dual-lens (Creator View + Engineer View) external GitHub repository analysis at Quick/Standard/Deep depths, with 9-phase pipeline including cloning, repomix, dimension wave, deep read, content eval, creator view, engineer view, value map, coverage audit, and tag suggestion.
sanitize_fields: home_repo_guard (jasonmichaelbell78-creator/sonash-v0 hardcoded in Critical Rule 5 and Guard Rail 2)
notes: Hardcoded home-repo guard: jasonmichaelbell78-creator/sonash-v0 MUST be sanitized for portability. TDMS system (MASTER_DEBT.jsonl) is opt-in via routing menu — sanitize but note. References both _shared/TAG_SUGGESTION.md and shared/CONVENTIONS.md — different directory names (shared/ vs _shared/) — shared/ exists at .claude/skills/shared/ with CONVENTIONS.md only; _shared/ is the main shared library. Not ported to JASON-OS. Has ARCHIVE.md companion. Version 5.0 with breaking phase renumber (4b→3.5). Invocation tracking uses scripts/reviews/write-invocation.ts (SoNash-specific). analysis.json validated by scripts/lib/analysis-schema.js. Reading-chain tracked in .research/reading-chain.jsonl. Cross-skill contract: preserve last_synthesized_at on analysis.json (v2.0 contract Session #284).
agent_types_spawned: dimension-agents (up to 4 concurrent for large repos)

name: website-analysis
purpose: Creator-first website analysis with superpowers-chrome extraction, compliance pre-flight, dual-lens Creator+Engineer Views at Quick/Standard/Deep depths, and multi-mode operation (Page/Site/Expedition/Cross-site).
external_refs: context7 MCP (referenced for library docs alternative)
sanitize_fields: TDMS routing menu option (opt-in, sanitize reference)
notes: superpowers-chrome is primary extractor — this is a SoNash-installed MCP server not available in JASON-OS. Graceful fallback to WebFetch + Playwright exists. References JASON-OS Domain 02a as consumer context — this is a SoNash-specific domain classification. Not ported to JASON-OS. Version 2.0 with same breaking phase renumber (4b→3.5) as repo-analysis. Self-audit dispatches code-reviewer agent on creator-view.md (Phase 6, step 6). Cross-skill contract: preserve last_synthesized_at on analysis.json. References _shared/TAG_SUGGESTION.md AND shared/CONVENTIONS.md (two different shared dirs). Invocation tracking uses scripts/reviews.

name: system-test
purpose: 23-domain interactive audit of the entire SoNash codebase with per-finding review, TDMS sync, multi-session support, and checkpoint recovery across 6 recommended sessions.
notes: Deeply SoNash-specific: audits the SoNash codebase across 23 domains (Cloud Functions, Firestore Rules, Auth, PWA, TDMS Integrity, etc.). All domain content references SoNash product code. TDMS system (MASTER_DEBT.jsonl) is a SoNash-specific technical debt management system. Not portable to JASON-OS — would require complete redefinition of all 23 domains for a different codebase. The interactive framework (per-finding review, suggestion/counter-argument pattern) IS potentially portable as a methodology. Output written to docs/audits/system-test/ (SoNash path). Companion domains.md contains the full 23-domain test plan with specific SoNash domain checks. Reference subdirectory with 3 docs: WORKFLOW.md, RECOVERY_PROCEDURES.md, TRIAGE_GUIDE.md. Version 4.0 introduced interactive per-finding review mode replacing the v3.1 parallel wave approach.

name: media-analysis
purpose: Content analysis of video and audio (YouTube, TikTok, podcasts, audio files) via transcription-first pipeline with dual-lens Creator+Engineer Views and 10-phase process.
notes: Whisper integration is opt-in and runtime-detected — graceful degradation well-designed. YouTube oEmbed fallback pattern documented. Windows Python bootstrap detail deferred to REFERENCE.md §3.1 (Windows-specific path editing for embedded Python ._pth file). Version 2.0 with same breaking phase renumber pattern as sibling CAS handlers. References JASON-OS as consumer (JASON-OS Domain 02a reference removed in v2.0 per T28 tagline removal). Not ported to JASON-OS. transcript.md is flagged as a non-CAS reusable artifact — notable design decision. Cross-skill contract: preserve last_synthesized_at. References _shared/TAG_SUGGESTION.md AND shared/CONVENTIONS.md.

name: audit-comprehensive
purpose: Orchestrate 9 specialized audit sub-skills (code, security, performance, documentation, refactoring, process, engineering-productivity, enhancements, ai-optimization) in staged waves with TDMS deduplication, interactive review, and AUDIT_TRACKER.md update.
notes: Depends on 9 sibling audit-* skills that are not inventoried in D1c scope (they are smaller skills). Agent teams mode requires CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 in settings.json. Uses episodic memory MCP (mcp__plugin_episodic-memory) for pre-flight context from past audit sessions — SoNash-specific MCP not in JASON-OS. SoNash-only: outputs to docs/audits/comprehensive/ and reads from docs/technical-debt/ — both SoNash product paths. Version 3.2 added mandatory MASTER_DEBT dedup step. AUDIT_TRACKER.md has 9 category tables (one per audit domain). Not portable without redefining all 9 audit sub-skills for target codebase. Reference subdir contains 3 docs at v1.0 from 2026-02-16 — older than SKILL.md v3.2. Future Enhancements section with 5 checklist items is explicitly deferred.

name: deep-research
purpose: Multi-agent research engine that decomposes questions, dispatches parallel searcher agents, synthesizes findings with citations and confidence levels, runs mandatory contrarian/OTB challenges, gap-pursuit verification, cross-model verification via Gemini CLI, and produces structured output with downstream adapters.
external_refs: gemini CLI (cross-model verification — optional, graceful degradation)
sanitize_fields: GSD adapter reference (gsd routing option — SoNash-specific pipeline)
notes: PORTED TO JASON-OS at v2.0 (same version). JASON-OS version has additional compatibility/metadata frontmatter fields not present in SoNash version. SoNash version lacks the agentskills-v1 compatibility field. GSD routing option (gsd:research-phase, gsd:new-project) present in SoNash — not in JASON-OS port. SoNash version has version history going back to v1.0 (2026-03-22); JASON-OS version Document Version is 2.0 / 2026-04-05 matching SoNash. REFERENCE.md is 22-section companion (1.5 version). domains/ subdirectory with technology.yaml, academic.yaml, business.yaml. Windows 0-byte agent output fallback documented (anthropics/claude-code#39791). Phase 2.5 persistence safety net added during piece-1a-discovery-scan-jason-os (T23 fix referenced in SKILL.md). research-plan-team.md used for L/XL research. No skills: frontmatter field.

name: document-analysis
purpose: Content analysis for PDFs, gists, articles, markdown, arxiv papers, code snippets, and meeting notes via dual-lens Creator+Engineer Views at Quick/Standard/Deep depths with 9-phase pipeline.
notes: Windows PDF fallback documented: if pdftoppm not found, use pdfjs-dist via node (npm install --no-save); permanent fix is winget install poppler. Created in Session #269 (T28 CAS). Version 2.0 has breaking phase renumber. Not ported to JASON-OS. Cross-skill contract: preserve last_synthesized_at on analysis.json. References _shared/TAG_SUGGESTION.md AND shared/CONVENTIONS.md. No agent spawning — all inline. Scope-explosion soft prompt at >100 pages PDF. 9-phase pipeline mirrors repo-analysis architecture.

name: skill-audit
purpose: Interactive behavioral quality audit for individual skills across 12 categories with single/batch/multi modes, evidence-based self-verification (grep + diff), and convergence-loop self-application.
notes: PORTED TO JASON-OS at v3.1 (JASON-OS) vs v4.0 (SoNash). JASON-OS version is one major version behind — SoNash v4.0 added batch/multi modes (D7-D20 decisions), cross-skill pattern detection (Phase 2.A), batched Phase 3. JASON-OS v3.1 only has single mode with gated interactive flow. The gap is significant: multi-skill batch auditing, Phase 2b findings-only flow, and Phase 2.A cross-skill patterns are SoNash-only features. REFERENCE.md references _shared/SELF_AUDIT_PATTERN.md. Invocation tracking uses scripts/reviews/write-invocation.ts. Phase 5.0 script (self-audit.js) is an external scripts/ dependency. Session #281 change: deterministic grep+diff verification replacing LLM agent layer. No skills: frontmatter field. Version 3.0 was a 42-decision self-audit rewrite.

name: artifacts-builder
purpose: Build elaborate multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts using React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + Parcel + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui, bundled to a single self-contained HTML file via scripts/bundle-artifact.sh.
notes: No version/compatibility frontmatter beyond name+description+license. LICENSE.txt present. The shadcn-components.tar.gz in scripts/ is a pre-bundled asset containing 40+ shadcn/ui components — this is binary content inside the skill dir (unusual). Scripts are shell scripts (bash), not Node.js. No _shared/ dependencies. Not ported to JASON-OS. Minimal skill — 5-step workflow, no phases, no state file, no self-audit. The anti-AI-slop design note (avoid purple gradients, centered layouts, Inter font) is a notable UX principle. Stack: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + Parcel + Tailwind CSS 3.4.1 + shadcn/ui.

name: skill-creator
purpose: 7-phase structured workflow for creating or updating skills — from discovery (6 categories, floor 12 questions) through approval-gated planning, build, structural+behavioral validation, mandatory skill-audit invocation, and closure with invocation tracking.
dependencies: convergence-loop (Phase 4.3 wiring + Phase 5 codebase claims verify)
sanitize_fields: EXTRACTIONS.md / extraction-journal.jsonl context scan in Phase 1 (project-specific artifacts)
notes: PORTED TO JASON-OS at v3.4 (same version as SoNash). Port appears current/matched. JASON-OS version has additional agentskills-v1 compatibility frontmatter. v1.0 note says 'Initial implementation (Anthropic skill, Apache 2.0)' — suggests this originated as an Anthropic-provided skill, not SoNash-native. License file present. Phase 1 extraction context scan (.research/EXTRACTIONS.md + extraction-journal.jsonl) requires those project artifacts to exist. References docs/agent_docs/SKILL_AGENT_POLICY.md — a SoNash product docs path. The Python scripts (init_skill.py, package_skill.py, quick_validate.py) are execution helpers. Phase 4.2 invokes init_skill.py as scaffold command. SKILL_STANDARDS.md and SELF_AUDIT_PATTERN.md in _shared/ are runtime dependencies for skill creation. No skills: frontmatter field.
agent_types_spawned: Explore agents (Phase 2 codebase scan for Complex tier)
convergence-loop quick preset (Phase 5 codebase claims verify)
